APP-00.title: What does 'lift 38×' mean?
APP-00.body: Lift is how many times more often a combination occurs vs random expectation. Lift > 20× is very strong, 5–20× is strong, 1–5× is weak, < 1× is anti-correlated. Hover the lift number anywhere in the app for the same explanation.
APP-00.tags: lift relate explanation tooltip

APP-00.title: Anomaly clusters: gl_mismatch, fraud_signal, unfamiliar, approver
APP-00.body: Anomalies are grouped by reason. Fraud signals (red header) show round-number amounts to weak-history vendors and call for compliance escalation. GL mismatches are likely data-entry errors. Unfamiliar patterns mean Aito has no strong precedent. Approver issues mean the routing pattern shifted.
APP-00.tags: anomaly fraud cluster category reasons recommendation

APP-00.title: Switching customers
APP-00.body: The customer dropdown in the topbar searches by id, tier, or name. The green dot means the customer's data is warmed in cache (instant load). Amber means cold — the first request takes 10–20 seconds while Aito computes predictions. Subsequent visits are cached for an hour.
APP-00.tags: customer dropdown switch warm cold cache

APP-00.title: What is the Data flow toggle?
APP-00.body: Click 'Data flow' in the topbar to overlay numbered gold badges on UI elements showing which Aito call produced each piece of data. The Aito side panel adds a 'Data flow on this page' section listing the calls. Every claim on screen traces to a specific query.
APP-00.tags: data flow tour debug audit trace

APP-01.title: Approval policy: invoices over €10,000

APP-01.title: Cost centre rules at Tornio Retail Oy Ab
APP-01.body: At Tornio Retail Oy Ab, cost centres are mapped strictly by department: Operations → CC-200, Sales → CC-300, IT → CC-400. Aito learns this from history but if the cost centre prediction looks wrong, override it — the next mining cycle will incorporate the correction.
APP-01.tags: cost centre department mapping override

APP-01.title: Vendor onboarding at Tornio Retail Oy Ab
APP-01.body: Before a new vendor's first invoice can be processed at Tornio Retail Oy Ab, procurement must complete the vendor checklist (Y-tunnus check, AML screening, IBAN verification). Aito's 'unfamiliar pattern' anomaly flags first-time vendors so they don't sneak through automation.

APP-04.title: How rules are mined
APP-04.body: Rules aren't hand-coded. The mine_rules_for_customer function calls _relate(vendor → gl_code) per customer and keeps patterns where support_ratio ≥ 0.95 with at least 5 matches. A new vendor automatically becomes a rule once it meets the threshold; a vendor that drops below loses its rule.

APP-04.title: Reading the touchless rate
APP-04.body: Touchless rate is the share of pending invoices Aito predicts at confidence ≥ 0.85. These are auto-routed without human review. Click the metric card to filter the table to only touchless invoices and confirm what's happening.

APP-05.title: Form Fill: confirming vs overriding predictions
APP-05.body: A predicted field is shown in italic gold = State 2. Tab or click outside to confirm; the field switches to confirmed green State 3. Press Esc to clear. Type to replace. The visual distinction matters — predicted-but-wrong values silently shipping is the most dangerous form-fill failure.
APP-05.tags: form fill smart predicted confirmed tab esc

APP-06.title: Payment matching via _predict invoice_id
APP-06.body: When a bank transaction arrives, Aito traverses the schema link from bank_transactions.invoice_id to invoices and ranks candidates by association with the description tokens and amount. No separate matching service or Levenshtein heuristic — just _predict.

LEGAL-03.title: GDPR and prediction logs
LEGAL-03.body: The prediction_log table contains user names (the approvers). Under GDPR this is personal data with legitimate-interest basis (audit trail of work activity). Right to erasure does NOT override the 6-year retention. Right of access does — user can request all log entries naming them.

LEGAL-07.title: Required invoice fields under Finnish VAT law
LEGAL-07.body: A valid Finnish invoice must contain: invoice date, sequential invoice number, supplier name + Y-tunnus + VAT number, buyer name + Y-tunnus, description of goods/services, taxable amount per VAT rate, VAT rate, total VAT, total amount due. Missing any of these makes the input VAT non-deductible.
LEGAL-07.tags: invoice fields y-tunnus vat finland required

CUST-0000-INT-01.title: Quarter-end close at Tornio Retail Oy Ab

CUST-0000-INT-01.title: Approval policy: invoices over €10,000
CUST-0000-INT-01.body: Tornio Retail Oy Ab requires dual approval for any invoice above €10,000. The approver's signature is recorded in the overrides table even when the prediction is correct, to preserve the SOX trail. For amounts above €50,000, the CFO must also sign.

APP-00.title: What does 'lift 38×' mean?
APP-00.body: Lift is how many times more often a combination occurs vs random expectation. Lift > 20× is very strong, 5–20× is strong, 1–5× is weak, < 1× is anti-correlated. Hover the lift number anywhere in the app for the same explanation.

APP-04.title: How rules are mined
APP-04.body: Rules aren't hand-coded. The mine_rules_for_customer function calls _relate(vendor → gl_code) per customer and keeps patterns where support_ratio ≥ 0.95 with at least 5 matches. APP-04.title: Reading the touchless rate
APP-04.body: Touchless rate is the share of pending invoices Aito predicts at confidence ≥ 0.85. These are auto-routed without human review. Click the metric card to filter the table to only touchless invoices and confirm what's happening.

LEGAL-07.title: Required invoice fields under Finnish VAT law
LEGAL-07.body: A valid Finnish invoice must contain: invoice date, sequential invoice number, supplier name + Y-tunnus + VAT number, buyer name + Y-tunnus, description of goods/services, taxable amount per VAT rate, VAT rate, total VAT, total amount due. Missing any of these makes the input VAT non-deductible.

APP-00.title: What does 'lift 38×' mean?
APP-00.body: Lift is how many times more often a combination occurs vs random expectation. Lift > 20× is very strong, 5–20× is strong, 1–5× is weak, < 1× is anti-correlated. Hover the lift number anywhere in the app for the same explanation.
APP-00.tags: lift relate explanation tooltip

APP-00.title: Anomaly clusters: gl_mismatch, fraud_signal, unfamiliar, approver
APP-00.body: Anomalies are grouped by reason. Fraud signals (red header) show round-number amounts to weak-history vendors and call for compliance escalation. GL mismatches are likely data-entry errors. Unfamiliar patterns mean Aito has no strong precedent. Approver issues mean the routing pattern shifted.
APP-00.tags: anomaly fraud cluster category reasons recommendation

APP-00.title: Switching customers
APP-00.body: The customer dropdown in the topbar searches by id, tier, or name. The green dot means the customer's data is warmed in cache (instant load). Amber means cold — the first request takes 10–20 seconds while Aito computes predictions. Subsequent visits are cached for an hour.
APP-00.tags: customer dropdown switch warm cold cache

APP-00.title: What is the Data flow toggle?
APP-00.body: Click 'Data flow' in the topbar to overlay numbered gold badges on UI elements showing which Aito call produced each piece of data. The Aito side panel adds a 'Data flow on this page' section listing the calls. Every claim on screen traces to a specific query.
APP-00.tags: data flow tour debug audit trace

APP-04.title: How rules are mined
APP-04.body: Rules aren't hand-coded. The mine_rules_for_customer function calls _relate(vendor → gl_code) per customer and keeps patterns where support_ratio ≥ 0.95 with at least 5 matches. A new vendor automatically becomes a rule once it meets the threshold; a vendor that drops below loses its rule.

APP-04.title: Reading the touchless rate
APP-04.body: Touchless rate is the share of pending invoices Aito predicts at confidence ≥ 0.85. These are auto-routed without human review. Click the metric card to filter the table to only touchless invoices and confirm what's happening.

LEGAL-02.title: Why a rule says 'Drifting'
LEGAL-02.body: A rule's status is Drifting when its precision over the last 12 weeks fell by more than 5 percentage points. Open the 12-week sparkline to see exactly when the drop happened. Common causes: vendor changed business model, new approver onboarded, override pattern emerging.
LEGAL-02.tags: drift drifting precision rule status

LEGAL-02.title: Override policy at Niemi Ventures Oy Ab
LEGAL-02.body: Niemi Ventures Oy Ab's policy requires the corrector to enter a reason for any GL or approver override — these surface in the Quality > Override Patterns view. Patterns repeating > 5 times with lift > 20× become rule candidates and are reviewed by the Controller monthly.
LEGAL-02.tags: override policy reason corrector pattern review

LEGAL-02.title: How rules are mined
LEGAL-02.body: Rules aren't hand-coded. The mine_rules_for_customer function calls _relate(vendor → gl_code) per customer and keeps patterns where support_ratio ≥ 0.95 with at least 5 matches. A new vendor automatically becomes a rule once it meets the threshold; a vendor that drops below loses its rule.
LEGAL-02.tags: rule mining relate support threshold

LEGAL-03.title: What does 'lift 38×' mean?
LEGAL-03.body: Lift is how many times more often a combination occurs vs random expectation. Lift > 20× is very strong, 5–20× is strong, 1–5× is weak, < 1× is anti-correlated. Hover the lift number anywhere in the app for the same explanation.

LEGAL-03.title: GDPR and prediction logs
LEGAL-03.body: The prediction_log table contains user names (the approvers). Under GDPR this is personal data with legitimate-interest basis (audit trail of work activity). Right to erasure does NOT override the 6-year retention. Right of access does — user can request all log entries naming them.

LEGAL-07.title: Required invoice fields under Finnish VAT law
LEGAL-07.body: A valid Finnish invoice must contain: invoice date, sequential invoice number, supplier name + Y-tunnus + VAT number, buyer name + Y-tunnus, description of goods/services, taxable amount per VAT rate, VAT rate, total VAT, total amount due. Missing any of these makes the input VAT non-deductible.
LEGAL-07.tags: invoice fields y-tunnus vat finland required

APP-05.title: Form Fill: confirming vs overriding predictions
APP-05.body: A predicted field is shown in italic gold = State 2. Tab or click outside to confirm; the field switches to confirmed green State 3. Press Esc to clear. Type to replace. The visual distinction matters — predicted-but-wrong values silently shipping is the most dangerous form-fill failure.

APP-06.title: Payment matching via _predict invoice_id
APP-06.body: When a bank transaction arrives, Aito traverses the schema link from bank_transactions.invoice_id to invoices and ranks candidates by association with the description tokens and amount. No separate matching service or Levenshtein heuristic — just _predict.

LEGAL-02.title: How rules are mined
LEGAL-02.body: Rules aren't hand-coded. The mine_rules_for_customer function calls _relate(vendor → gl_code) per customer and keeps patterns where support_ratio ≥ 0.95 with at least 5 matches. A new vendor automatically becomes a rule once it meets the threshold; a vendor that drops below loses its rule.

LEGAL-03.title: GDPR and prediction logs
LEGAL-03.body: The prediction_log table contains user names (the approvers). Under GDPR this is personal data with legitimate-interest basis (audit trail of work activity). Right to erasure does NOT override the 6-year retention. Right of access does — user can request all log entries naming them.

LEGAL-07.title: Required invoice fields under Finnish VAT law
LEGAL-07.body: A valid Finnish invoice must contain: invoice date, sequential invoice number, supplier name + Y-tunnus + VAT number, buyer name + Y-tunnus, description of goods/services, taxable amount per VAT rate, VAT rate, total VAT, total amount due. Missing any of these makes the input VAT non-deductible.

APP-01.tags: cost centre department mapping override

APP-02.title: Quarter-end close at Tornio Insurance Group Oy
APP-02.body: Tornio Insurance Group Oy closes the books on the 5th business day of the following month. Run Quality > Rules > Snapshot before close to lock the rule set in effect during the quarter. The snapshot becomes part of the audit support pack.
APP-02.tags: close month quarter end snapshot audit

APP-02.title: Approval policy: invoices over €10,000
APP-02.body: Tornio Insurance Group Oy requires dual approval for any invoice above €10,000. The approver's signature is recorded in the overrides table even when the prediction is correct, to preserve the SOX trail. For amounts above €50,000, the CFO must also sign.
APP-02.tags: approval policy threshold dual cfo over

APP-02.title: Vendor onboarding at Tornio Insurance Group Oy
APP-02.body: Before a new vendor's first invoice can be processed at Tornio Insurance Group Oy, procurement must complete the vendor checklist (Y-tunnus check, AML screening, IBAN verification). Aito's 'unfamiliar pattern' anomaly flags first-time vendors so they don't sneak through automation.
APP-02.tags: vendor onboarding checklist procurement first-time

APP-02.title: Switching customers
APP-02.body: The customer dropdown in the topbar searches by id, tier, or name. The green dot means the customer's data is warmed in cache (instant load). Amber means cold — the first request takes 10–20 seconds while Aito computes predictions. Subsequent visits are cached for an hour.
APP-02.tags: customer dropdown switch warm cold cache

APP-03.title: What does 'lift 38×' mean?
APP-03.body: Lift is how many times more often a combination occurs vs random expectation. Lift > 20× is very strong, 5–20× is strong, 1–5× is weak, < 1× is anti-correlated. Hover the lift number anywhere in the app for the same explanation.

APP-03.title: Why a rule says 'Drifting'
APP-03.body: A rule's status is Drifting when its precision over the last 12 weeks fell by more than 5 percentage points. Open the 12-week sparkline to see exactly when the drop happened. Common causes: vendor changed business model, new approver onboarded, override pattern emerging.

APP-04.title: How rules are mined
APP-04.body: Rules aren't hand-coded. The mine_rules_for_customer function calls _relate(vendor → gl_code) per customer and keeps patterns where support_ratio ≥ 0.95 with at least 5 matches. A new vendor automatically becomes a rule once it meets the threshold; a vendor that drops below loses its rule.

APP-04.title: Reading the touchless rate
APP-04.body: Touchless rate is the share of pending invoices Aito predicts at confidence ≥ 0.85. These are auto-routed without human review. Click the metric card to filter the table to only touchless invoices and confirm what's happening.

APP-05.title: Form Fill: confirming vs overriding predictions
APP-05.body: A predicted field is shown in italic gold = State 2. Tab or click outside to confirm; the field switches to confirmed green State 3. Press Esc to clear. Type to replace. The visual distinction matters — predicted-but-wrong values silently shipping is the most dangerous form-fill failure.
APP-05.tags: form fill smart predicted confirmed tab esc

APP-06.title: Payment matching via _predict invoice_id
APP-06.body: When a bank transaction arrives, Aito traverses the schema link from bank_transactions.invoice_id to invoices and ranks candidates by association with the description tokens and amount. No separate matching service or Levenshtein heuristic — just _predict.

LEGAL-03.title: GDPR and prediction logs
LEGAL-03.body: The prediction_log table contains user names (the approvers). Under GDPR this is personal data with legitimate-interest basis (audit trail of work activity). Right to erasure does NOT override the 6-year retention. Right of access does — user can request all log entries naming them.

LEGAL-07.title: Required invoice fields under Finnish VAT law
LEGAL-07.body: A valid Finnish invoice must contain: invoice date, sequential invoice number, supplier name + Y-tunnus + VAT number, buyer name + Y-tunnus, description of goods/services, taxable amount per VAT rate, VAT rate, total VAT, total amount due. Missing any of these makes the input VAT non-deductible.
LEGAL-07.tags: invoice fields y-tunnus vat finland required
